text:
Pb-free relaxors with high capacitive energy storage wrec and efficiency η are urgently needed for advanced pulsed power applications. While local compositional heterogeneity in the structure of solid solution relaxors is responsible for the complexity of the polar state, its application to energy storage has not been fully explored. In this work, we demonstrate that high wrec = 15.2 J cm−3 and ultrahigh efficiency η = 91% can be simultaneously achieved in a model system of lead-free Bi0.5Na0.5TiO3–BaTiO3 relaxor ceramics by designing local chemical clustering.

Local structural and polar ordering in Ba0.6Bi0.4Ti1-xNxs (BNT) ceramics: Atomic scale investigations The existence of sub-nanometer-sized clusters of Bi, Na and Ba species along with their respective polar displacements have been demonstrated using the three dimensional (3D) atomistic model obtained from neutron/X-ray total scattering and reverse Monte Carlo simulations validated using density functional theory calculations. These structural features are associated with the polar state of relaxors characterized by the existence of small-sized polar clusters and large fluctuations in the magnitudes and directions of unit cell polar vector, which leads to high polarizability at high fields, low remnant polarization and coercive fields, high breakdown electric field, an unusual field dependence of electric current and excellent energy storage characteristics such as low remnant polarization, low coercive field, high breakdown electric field and excellent cycle stability along with high discharge energies, thereby indicating the role of hidden local order in the polarization behavior of relaxors ceramics and their utilization in energy storage applications.
